articles.title: $24K worth of cocaine seized, 6 people charged in GTA drug trafficking investigation
articles.summary: Six people have been charged in connection with an investigation into cocaine trafficking in York Region and the Greater Toronto Area. York Regional Police began investigating in 2025 after officers became aware of a person involved in trafficking controlled substances and firearms in York Region and surrounding areas. Investigators identified more suspects over several months, […]

articles.title: Receipt left inside allegedly stolen SUV leads Hamilton police to 16‑year‑old suspect
articles.summary: Hamilton police say a seemingly ordinary fast‑food receipt left inside a stolen vehicle helped officers track down and arrest a 16‑year‑old boy in a theft investigation earlier this week. Officers were called around 7:50 a.m. on Monday, July 21, to the area of Hwy. 8 and King Street East after reports of an abandoned black […]

articles.title: Brampton-based Roshel in the mix as Canada narrows search for armoured vehicle fleet
articles.summary: As Canada gears up to spend 2 per cent of its GDP on defence by the end of this fiscal year, it’s investing nearly $5 billion in a new fleet of armoured and unarmoured land utility vehicles. CityNews has learned only two bids remain on the table and one of them is local. Roshel has […]

articles.title: SickKids doctor’s licence suspended after arrest on child sex abuse material charges
articles.summary: The organization that regulates Ontario’s medical profession says it has suspended the licence of a doctor at Toronto’s Hospital for Sick Children who was arrested Tuesday on charges related to child sexual abuse and exploitation material. The College of Physicians and Surgeons’ website says Dilan Dissanayake is not permitted to practise medicine, adding an interim […]

articles.title: Man seeks answers after mystery object appears to fall from sky and crash into car on Hwy. 427
articles.summary: An 18-year-old driver had an encounter with a UFO on Highway 427 earlier this month. In this case, though, UFO stands for Unidentified ‘Falling’ Object. Ayden Koo was driving in the northbound lanes of the busy highway near Finch Avenue on July 11 at around 5:30 p.m. when an object appeared to fall from the […]

articles.title: Fire at Scarborough commercial plaza being investigated as suspicious, authorities say
articles.summary: What started as a vehicle fire spread to a commercial plaza near Eglinton Avenue E. and Kennedy Road early Wednesday morning, Toronto Fire says.

articles.title: How wildfire smoke travels from Ontario to New York City — and turns distant city skies orange
articles.summary: Wildfires that have torched huge areas of forest and devastated communities are also sending smoke far and wide, prompting air quality warnings in distant cities and heated political rhetoric from the U.S. Experts say there's nothing particularly unusual about this summer's smoke patterns — but they say wildfires are getting worse, and that's not likely to stop on either side of the border.

articles.title: Woman, 18, charged with attempted murder after teen stabbed on bus: Peel police
articles.summary: An 18-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder after a teen was stabbed on board a transit bus in Mississauga, Ont, police say.

articles.title: Toronto's Pearson airport launching 2nd phase of multi-billion dollar construction project
articles.summary: Officials at Toronto’s Pearson International Airport launched the second phase of a multi-billion dollar construction project aimed at modernizing and expanding the facility on Tuesday.

articles.title: Toronto step closer to 'much needed' Liberty Village traffic and community additions
articles.summary: The City of Toronto has voted on plans to update Liberty Village's infrastructure, including a new community center, Canada Post service and school development.
